memoryArtifacts.headline: Family of Herbert from Annals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777
memoryArtifacts.description: Family of Herbert from Annuals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777. However, it is noted, that Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) was not the Count of Vermandois, but rather his uncle was, Herbert IV, Count of Vermandois. It is also noted that the son of Herbertus Camerarius, Henry FitzHerbert (Henry the Treasurer) is missing from the Annals and Antiquities, but his grandson Herbert (Herbert FitzHenry) and great grandson Herbert  (Herbert FitzHerbert) are included.

popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description: Miles FitzWalter of Gloucester, 1st Earl of Hereford (died 24 December 1143) (alias Miles of Gloucester[1]) was a great magnate based in the west of England. He was hereditary Constable of England[a] …

sourceList.title: MILES of GLOUCESTER, son and heir (apparently only son) of WALTER OF GLOUCESTER, hereditary Sheriff of Gloucester and probably constable, received from Henry I in 1121 Sibyl, daughter of Bernard DE NEUFMARCHÉ, in marriage, with all the lands of her father
Miles Fitz Walter, "Find A Grave Index"
The family of Herbert in Dugdale’s The Baronage of England, pg. 625

zh.lifeSummary: Miles Fitzwalter Of Gloucester 1st Earl Of Hereford是Walter FitzRoger de Pitres的兒子，於大約1082年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾格盧斯特。他於1112年5月與Sybil de Neufmarché結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1143年12月24日在英國格盧斯特斯爾科華士 本 迪恩享年62歲，埋葬在威爾士蒙姆爾利藍尼 普利馬 普利奧利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Miles Fitzwalter Of Gloucester 1st Earl Of Hereford是Walter FitzRoger de Pitres的儿子，于大约1082年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾格盧斯特。他于1112年5月与Sybil de Neufmarché结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1143年12月24日在英国格盧斯特斯爾科華士 本 迪恩享年62岁，埋葬在威尔士蒙姆爾利藍尼 普利馬 普利奧利。

nameMeanings.name: Fitzwalter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , ‘son of Walter’.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Walter
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Miles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Norman origin but uncertain derivation. Unlike most Norman names it is, as far as can be ascertained, not derived from any known Old French or Germanic name element. It may be a greatly altered pet form of Michael , which came to be associated with the Latin word miles ‘soldier’ because of the military attributes of the archangel Michael. However, the usual Latin form of the name in the Middle Ages was Milo. There is a common Slavic name element mil ‘grace, favour’, with which it may possibly have some connection. The name has been in regular use in England since the 16th century. See also Milo and Myles .

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Miles Fitzwalter Of Gloucester 1st Earl Of Hereford was born about 1082, in Gloucester, Gloucestershire, England as the son of Walter FitzRoger de Pitres. He married Sybil de Neufmarché in May 1112. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 24 December 1143, in Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 62, and was buried in Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: Miles Fitzwalter Of Gloucester 1st Earl Of Hereford est né environ 1082, à Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, fils de Walter FitzRoger de Pitres. Il a épousé Sybil de Neufmarché en mai 1112. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 24 décembre 1143, à Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Miles Fitzwalter Of Gloucester 1st Earl Of Hereford wurde um 1082 in Gloucester, Gloucestershire, England, als Sohn von Walter FitzRoger de Pitres geboren. Er hat im Mai 1112 Sybil de Neufmarché geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 24. Dezember 1143 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire, England und wurde in Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Wales begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Miles Fitzwalter Of Gloucester 1st Earl Of Hereford è nato il circa 1082 nel Gloucester, Gloucestershire, England come figlio di Walter FitzRoger de Pitres. Lui sposato Sybil de Neufmarché nel maggio 1112. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 24 dicembre 1143, nel Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: Miles Fitzwalter Of Gloucester 1st Earl Of Herefordは約1082年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアグロウセスターでWalter FitzRoger de Pitresの息子として生まれた。1112年5月にSybil de Neufmarchéと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1143年12月24日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフォーエスト オフ ディーンで62歳で死去し，ウェールズモンモウスシュアイアランソニー プリマ プリオリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Miles Fitzwalter Of Gloucester 1st Earl Of Hereford(은)는 대략 1082년 영국 그루스스터실르 그루스스터에서 Walter FitzRoger de Pitres의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1112년 5월 Sybil de Neufmarché(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1143년 12월 24일 영국 그루스스터실르 포레스트 프 디에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스 몬모우스실르 랜서니 플리마 플리오리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Miles Fitzwalter Of Gloucester 1st Earl Of Hereford nasceu aproximadamente 1082, em Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra filho de Walter FitzRoger de Pitres. Ele casou-se com Sybil de Neufmarché em maio de 1112. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de dezembro de 1143, em Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Gales.

ru.lifeSummary: Miles Fitzwalter Of Gloucester 1st Earl Of Hereford родился около 1082 г. в местности глоусестир, глоусестиршир, Англия. Его отца звали Walter FitzRoger de Pitres. Он женился на женщине по имени Sybil de Neufmarché май 1112 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 24 декабря 1143 г. в местности форест оф дин, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности ллантони прима приори, монмоутшир, Уэльс.

es.lifeSummary: Miles Fitzwalter Of Gloucester 1st Earl Of Hereford nació aproximadamente 1082, en Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra como hijo de Walter FitzRoger de Pitres. Se casó con Sybil de Neufmarché en mayo de 1112. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 24 de diciembre de 1143, en Forest of Dean, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Llanthony Prima Priory, Monmouthshire, Gales.
